title: Comparatives and superlatives
explanation:
Comparatives and superlatives (A2)

Short adjectives: cheap → cheaper → the cheapest; big → bigger → the biggest

Long adjectives: expensive → more expensive → the most expensive

Irregular: good → better → the best; bad → worse → the worst

Use than with comparatives: She is taller than me.

questions.text: This hotel is [*more expensive|expensiver|most expensive] than that one.
It was [*the worst|worse|bad] film I've ever seen.
My room is [*bigger|biggest|more big] than yours.
She is [*the best|better|good] student in the class.
English is [*easier|easiest|more easy] than Chinese for me.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: London is ____ than my town. | *busier | busiest | more busy
That was ____ meal of the holiday. | *the nicest | nicer | nice
He runs ____ than his brother. | *faster | fastest | more fast
This exercise is ____ than the last one. | *more difficult | difficulter | most difficult
Mount Everest is [*the highest|higher|high] mountain in the world.
Her car is [*older|oldest|more old] than mine.
This is [*the most interesting|more interesting|interestinger] book here.
My flat is [*smaller|smallest|more small] but it's cheaper.
